name: Kit
price_basis: Creator plan for up to 1,000 subscribers, monthly billing, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: TRIM: the free plan handles 10,000 subscribers. Stop paying until unlimited sequences earn their seat.
what_zo_builds: A private newsletter desk that stores subscribers, tags, drafts, and sequence rules in workspace files, then sends through a provider you control. Zo can replace forms, landing pages, basic sequences, and the dashboard. It should not pretend to replace bulk-email deliverability infrastructure.
dont_kill_if: Your paid automations directly sell products, or Kit's deliverability and recommendation network produce more than the subscription costs. Downgrade first. Do not move a revenue-producing list on principle.
build_brief: Goal: remove my paid Kit plan without gambling with my list. Export subscribers, tags, forms, sequences, and unsubscribe state. Build a private /newsletter route that imports the export, preserves consent and tags, drafts broadcasts, and runs my basic sequences. Keep a real bulk-email provider for delivery. Start on Kit's free Newsletter plan while I compare delivery results. Done when every form and sequence has a tested replacement, unsubscribe handling works, and two sends match or beat my current delivery numbers.
